instructions: Focus on proof correctness, verification-boundary safety, version drift, and regressions in negative/tamper-path coverage. Ignore style-only issues and generated artifacts unless they create a reproducibility or integrity risk.

rules.rule: Flag any change that can let a proof, artifact manifest, or decoding-chain step verify under weaker conditions than before, including weaker commitment checks, missing nested-proof verification, or mismatched carried-state derivation.
When proof semantics, carried-state structure, version constants, or manifest schemas change, require at least one updated negative, tamper, or compatibility test. Flag happy-path-only changes.
Flag documentation or README claims that no longer match the actual repository behavior, supported phases, backend boundaries, or reproducibility instructions.
Flag CI or script changes that reduce matrix coverage, weaken artifact determinism, or silently skip required proof and backend combinations.
